summary: PARTIALLY_IMPLEMENTED. HEAD 545dec476ac2bce7e8c82a0a21e307ea2341874e is identical to origin/main (zero commits ahead/behind). Current main contains the earlier worker tracing/log-correlation baseline from #1036 and durable incident trace references, but repository searches and recent merge history show no implementation of #2559’s shared OTLP bootstrap, API instrumentation, complete Temporal propagation, domain/Omnigent spans, configured trace/log deep links, collector-outage handling, full-path collector integration test, or exporter performance evidence. No long test suites were run because this step is read-only inspection and the missing structural artifacts are decisive.

requirements.description: One API-started workflow yields one exported navigable trace across Temporal, activities, provider/managed-runtime or Omnigent, and artifacts: worker-only TracingInterceptor exists in moonmind/workflows/temporal/worker_runtime.py, but no application OTLP exporter or full-path trace journey exists.

Every API and worker process uses a shared telemetry bootstrap with stable resource attributes: worker_runtime.py assigns a worker service resource, but no shared bootstrap used by API, auxiliary services, and local tooling exists.
All Temporal client construction paths use propagation interceptors: repository evidence only wires TracingInterceptor into worker construction; client paths do not consistently install it.
Continue-As-New and child workflows preserve logical correlation while separating physical run trace segments: no OpenTelemetry propagation/link contract or corresponding tests were found.

Workflow Detail and incident/control surfaces expose authorized configured trace/log deep links or an explicit unavailable state: deterministic incident trace references exist, but no backend URL templates or operator-facing configured deep links were found.

Managed direct-compatibility and Omnigent bridge spans share MoonMind correlation fields: managed-session context is added to worker logs, but no instrumented Omnigent/direct-compatibility span contract was found.

Sensitive or unbounded payloads are excluded from spans/logs by default: existing structured logging and bounded artifact references support the policy, but no shared span attribute sanitizer/payload-capture contract tests cover the end-to-end telemetry path.

Collector/backend failure is fail-open and visible through bounded health metrics: no configured application exporter, exporter-health metrics, or collector-outage failure-injection evidence was found.

Span names/attributes, metric labels, and redaction behavior have automated contract tests: worker log correlation tests exist, but end-to-end span attribute, metric-label, and redaction contracts do not.
A hermetic integration test queries a test collector and proves full-path trace continuity and required attributes: no test collector configuration or such journey test was found.
Performance tests document telemetry latency, CPU/memory overhead, and exporter queue behavior: no exporter performance evidence was found.
